channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Valve Axes Steam Machine ‘Companion Cube’: ‘We Should Have Asked First’
item.content:encoded:
Valve has silenced an attempt to create a ‘Companion Cube’ skin for the Steam Machine, shutting down appeals and refusing to give any blessing for a third-party company to create it moving forward. This axes an attempt by dbrand to honor the arrival of the Steam Machine by creating a beloved Portal-themed throwback skin for it.

The problem was that dbrand never sought permission to create the skin, and when it launched, Valve’s legal team spread across the product like wildfire, killing it in no time. Despite dbrand’s appeals, Valve refused to relent, standing firm and expressly forbidding the company to create the skin.

Companion Cube Skin for Steam Machine Eliminated

Dbrand revealed that when the Companion Cube ‘wrap’ launched for the Steam Machine on June 22nd, it became the second-fastest-selling product they’d ever created, and that it had arrived after months of painstaking development and iteration.

In a post on social media, the team explained:

We poured our souls into this project. More than a thousand hours went into engineering from our industrial design team. Forty-four sets of injection molding tools were developed, one for each of the cube’s sub-components. The entire product was redesigned from scratch more than once, just to get the way it cradles the console exactly right.

We literally rented out a university campus to film the launch video. By the end, we were losing money on every $99 Poverty Cube sold, but it didn’t matter. This had turned into a passion project for the entire organization.

Now, every customer who purchased a Companion Cube will get a refund.

Responding to Valve’s legal issues, dbrand immediately ‘eviscerated’ the entire presence of the Companion Cube from the internet, incinerating all traces of it to avoid further ramifications.

Ultimately, the team admitted that Valve had done nothing wrong by closing down the skin; it was more dbrand’s fault for not ‘asking first’ if they could create the Companion Cube.

Would you have bought the warp for the Steam Machine if it had been allowed to stay on the market? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the new Cyberpunk Edgerunners 2 teaser revealing the release window. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dave the Diver iOS and Android Release Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
Dave the Diver’s developers have confirmed that the title will be available on mobile soon; pre-registrations are now live.

Fans of Dave the Diver have been treated to numerous surprises this month. The new DLC, In the Jungle, was released, and Dave has also made his way into Fortnite. But that’s not all; the developers have one final announcement regarding a platform expansion for this popular title.

Dave the Diver iOS Listing Seemingly Reveals Mobile Release Date

The iOS and Android listings for Dave the Diver are already out for players to pre-register for the game. However, it seems the game’s release date has been revealed in the iOS listing, as it states that the game is ‘Expected’ on August 27, 2026. The game will be available to download for free, but the full version will cost $9.99.

The iOS listing also provides several other details for the game, such as the size, which will be 3.3 GB. Players have also been informed of the compatibility requirements: for iPhone, iPad, and iPod, they will need iOS 13.0 or later. Mac users would require macOS 11.0 with the Apple M1 chip or later, and it’s also supported on the Apple Vision headset, which requires visionOS 1.0 or later.

For those unaware, in Dave the Diver, players dive and explore the depths of Blue Hole during the day and run an exotic sushi restaurant at night. The Blue Hole depths are filled with a variety of mysteries, along with several fish to catch and creatures to encounter. You will also have several weapons at your disposal, but keep an eye on the oxygen meter, as once it runs out, all collected items will be left behind.

The title features some intriguing characters that accompany a story filled with jokes, spoofs, and other humor. It features a pixel-based art style with over 200 kinds of sea creatures. There are also minigames, side quests, and multiple storylines that players can explore in the game.

What are your thoughts on Dave the Diver coming to Android and iOS? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the new Cyberpunk Edgerunners 2 teaser revealing the release window. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
